quarter: Q4 FY2026 (fiscal year ended ~early February 2026; reported March 5, 2026)

keyMetrics.revenue: $2.219B for Q4 FY2026 (+22% YoY), a record; full-year FY2026 revenue $8.195B (+42% YoY)
keyMetrics.dataCenterRevenue: FY2026 data center revenue ~$6.1B (~74% of total, up ~46% YoY); Q2 FY2026 data center was $1.49B (+69% YoY)
keyMetrics.epsNonGAAP: $0.80 non-GAAP diluted EPS in Q4 FY2026 ($0.46 GAAP); full-year FY2026 non-GAAP EPS $2.84, up 81% YoY
keyMetrics.guidanceNextQ: Management guided FY2027 revenue toward ~$11.5B with YoY growth expected to accelerate each quarter, driven by data center; Q4 revenue exceeded the December 2, 2025 guidance. Marvell subsequently reported record Q1 FY2027 revenue of ~$2.418B (+27% YoY).

aiCommentary: Management said robust AI demand drove record FY2026 results, with custom silicon scaling from effectively zero to roughly a quarter of data-center revenue across 18+ cloud design wins and 50+ active custom-AI opportunities with 10+ customers. CEO Matt Murphy highlighted that fiscal 2026 design wins hit an all-time record and bookings were growing at a record pace heading into FY2027. Marvell reiterated multi-year targets of FY2027 revenue ~$11.5B, FY2028 ~$16.5B, and a goal of >$10B in custom-business revenue by fiscal 2029, with custom programs (e.g., AWS Trainium, Microsoft Maia) ramping in volume.
demandCommentary: Demand was led overwhelmingly by the AI data center segment (~74% of FY2026 revenue), with management expecting data-center-driven YoY growth to accelerate each quarter in FY2027. Outside data center, enterprise networking and carrier infrastructure were recovering off cyclical troughs (Q2 FY2026: enterprise networking +28% YoY, carrier +71% YoY off low bases), consumer was lumpy (+30% YoY in Q2), and automotive/industrial was roughly flat and reduced after the Infineon automotive-Ethernet divestiture.

keyQuotes.speaker: Matt Murphy (CEO)
keyQuotes.quote: Marvell delivered record fiscal 2026 revenue of $8.195 billion, growing 42% year-over-year, driven by robust AI demand.
keyQuotes.source: Seeking Alpha (Q4 FY2026 earnings call transcript)

keyQuotes.speaker: Matt Murphy (CEO)
keyQuotes.quote: We expect year-over-year revenue growth to accelerate each quarter in fiscal 2027, driven by continued strength in our data center business, with bookings continuing to grow at a record pace.
keyQuotes.source: Seeking Alpha (Q4 FY2026 earnings call transcript)
